summary: The built-in adoption: `skill` templates its bundled reference documents at the directory's markdown, and `supporting-doc` ships as their prose-only kind

edit.description: `skill` (142-158) declares no template. Give it its bundled reference documents as one file-child layer â€” `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16). New `supporting-doc` kind beside it: fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename, nested-file locus. Its default contract ships **empty** per the standing bar ("Default contracts"); its one clause is SUPPORTING-DOC-REACH-CLAUSE's to add once an incidence clause outside a requirement is admissible, never this entry's to invent. The kind ships without joining the surface enumeration, the `requirement` precedent. Rider (open-questions): the doc-comment cites at 565/611 name two deleted `packages/{rule,memory}.anthropic/PACKAGE.md` files â€” discharge them while here.

The engine-side reader mirror of the shipped kinds (the `CustomKind::new` calls and `definitions`): `skill`'s row grows its template, and `supporting-doc` enters as the child kind a discovered document is read as â€” 91c288c landed the `CustomKind::nested_file` constructor (src/kind.rs:600) this row is the first built-in caller of. Rider (open-questions): the narration cites here are dated @07-07 / @07-10 or undated (85, 194/222, 63/106) â€” every fact still holds; re-verify against the live docs and bump the dates while here.

The embedded built-in lock re-derives once `skill`'s row carries a template and `supporting-doc` has a row â€” the first built-in to declare either (91c288c left this file byte-identical because no built-in declared the locus; that changes here).
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the two grown rows.
`skill`'s declaration row carries its reference-document template, and `supporting-doc`'s row carries its nested-file locus and empty field schema.

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `skill`'s kind row grows its `templates` column (child kind `supporting-doc`, the markdown path pattern), and `supporting-doc` enters as a new kind row at the nested-file locus.

tests.asserts: `skill` declares one file-child layer of `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, and `supporting-doc` is fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically from the SDK with the two grown rows
a real skill directory's markdown document is discovered as that skill's `supporting-doc` child

acceptance: A skill's bundled reference documents are nested file members of the same skill, typed `supporting-doc` (builtins.md, "The shipped kinds"), their path the skill template's declared fact â€” nesting-is-model-containment / locus-is-serialization stops being an unbacked claim. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes 0027 (abe5d5d), Consequences half 3 â€” the built-in adoption; 0027 says it re-enters buildable, and it does, minus the clause. The template claims the honest markdown subset: supporting files of other types stay unmodeled and named as such (0027's second Rejected bullet), the `settings.json` partial-governance posture. Discharges the `supportingDocs()` factory asymmetry in open-questions. Gate re-tested at b745415: NESTED-FILE-DISCOVERY unshipped. scoped at 4ba483f.

summary: The fixture-harness wiring builder belongs in `tests/common`, not copy-pasted per suite: three copies of tmpdir â†’ `.temper` â†’ `harness.ts` â†’ `vendor_sdk` collapse into one home

edit.description: The declared one home for test scaffolding â€” its own header (1-4) names "the SDK vendoring used by tests that drive a real `node` subprocess" as the copy-per-suite it exists to end. It carries every ingredient (`tmpdir` 31, `scaffold` 44, `vendor_sdk` 96) and not the builder that composes them into a fixture harness, which is exactly the surface the three copies below each re-derive. Add that builder: a label plus the fixture program text, returning `(harness, into)`. `tests/install.rs`'s `vendor_sdk` calls are a different job â€” the install verb scaffolds its own `harness.ts` under test, rather than wiring an authored fixture program â€” and are out of scope.

`wire_sdk_harness_program` (1223) is the original, and the surface the other two copies' doc comments cite by name as the pattern they follow; `wire_sdk_harness` (1217) binds it to this suite's `HARNESS_PROGRAM`. Retire the local body onto the common home â€” the `HARNESS_PROGRAM` binding stays suite-local, being this suite's fixture rather than scaffolding.

`wire_memberless_harness` (61-71): a verbatim copy of emit.rs's builder â€” tmpdir, `.temper`, write `harness.ts`, `vendor_sdk` â€” whose own doc (58-60) cites `tests/emit.rs`'s `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it follows. Call the common builder with `MEMBERLESS_BUILTIN_PROGRAM`.

`wire_waypoint_harness` (141, shifted +24 by 91c288c's nested-file-child seam case), the third copy (e76ec85), carrying the same doc cite back to emit.rs's builder. Call the common builder with `WAYPOINT_PROGRAM`.

acceptance: One builder composes tmpdir â†’ `.temper` â†’ `harness.ts` â†’ `vendor_sdk`, in `tests/common`, and no suite re-derives it (engineering.md, "One job, one home": shared fixtures and builders live in one home, never copy-pasted per file). Behavior is unchanged, so the three suites' existing tests are the coverage; `cargo test` green.
notes: Found sweeping 74f4e62..ca4e866: e76ec85 added the third copy, its doc citing emit.rs's `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it followed rather than the home it should have extended â€” the copy-per-suite `tests/common`'s own header says it exists to end. Serialized behind the chain: shares projection_path_seam.rs with the nested-file work, builtin_lock_frozen.rs with SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS. Re-verified at b745415: all three copies live. rescoped at b745415.

summary: `supporting-doc`'s reach clause: a supporting file is invisible unless its host skill references it â€” the by-kind universal binding, each-grain incidence, advisory

edit.description: `supporting-doc`'s default contract â€” the empty exported clause array SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS ships â€” gains its one clause: an incoming-degree bound over the kind's members, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16), the vendor fact that every supporting file is invisible unless SKILL.md references it. **Universal, not existential**: the fact holds of every supporting document, so the spelling is the by-kind binding with each-grain incidence (`contract.md`, "selection"), never a requirement â€” a requirement is the opt-in selector and would make a vendor fact consumer ceremony. **Reach is locus-agnostic**: any resolved edge from the host skill counts, post-0025's one edge noun and one degree semantics â€” a mention included, this clause being the "a contract may" that mention's obligation-free default reserves (`contract.md`, "edge"). Severity **advisory**, per `intent.md` invariant 5: a shipped coverage clause enters advisory, and escalation is the corpus's declared act.
The embedded built-in lock re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains its one clause row.
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the grown clause row.
`supporting-doc`'s default contract carries exactly one clause, at advisory severity, cited and guided â€” the shipped-clause bar (`builtins.md`, "The clauses live in code").

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains one clause row (the incoming-degree reach bound, advisory).

tests.asserts: `supporting-doc`'s default contract is one advisory reach clause, cited to the vendor fact and carrying its guidance
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically with the clause row

acceptance: A supporting document no edge from its host skill reaches is an advisory finding naming the vendor fact (builtins.md, "Default contracts"; the clause is decidable, severity-declared, guided, cited and dated) â€” and reach counts any resolved edge, locus-agnostic. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 ruling's clause itself (25e9317 resolved the fork). The single-tag gate names PREDICATE-SELECTION-ALGEBRA â€” without it an incidence clause outside a requirement is inadmissible, so the clause cannot ship. It ALSO requires SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS: that entry mints the kind and its empty contract, and shares builtins.ts / builtin_lock.toml / builtin_lock_frozen.rs â€” so this is the queue tail, behind both chains. scoped at d572827.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binaryâ†”npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) â€” no signing creds â€” but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead â€” release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested at b745415: no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, release.yml:7-9 still defers darwin. scoped at b676656.
